summary: The Register reported cybersecurity vendor Arctic Wolf laid off 250 workers in a restructuring the company said would help it operate more efficiently and continue investing in its Superintelligence platform and agentic Security Operations Center.
evidenceQuote: While these decisions are difficult, they position Arctic Wolf to operate more efficiently, continue investing in our Superintelligence platform and Agentic SOC, and deliver strong value to customers.
notes: The Register reported the layoffs represented less than 10% of Arctic Wolf's workforce and appeared to affect sales, product development, and marketing. Because the company spokesperson explicitly tied the restructuring to continued investment in its AI-oriented Superintelligence platform and Agentic SOC, this is classified as explicit AI cited.

sources.name: The Register
sources.quote: Cybersecurity vendor Arctic Wolf has laid off 250 workers in a restructuring that it says is designed to position the company to invest more in AI through its superintelligence platform and agentic Security Operations Center (SOC), a company spokesperson told The Register.
